body:
I will be speaking directly to YOU. |

Friend, Tonight at 9 PM EST I’m going to speak before Congress for the first time. But more importantly, I will be speaking to you -- the American people. My speech will be a message of optimism, hope, and love for the greatest country in history. I will lay out our agenda for a stronger, freer, and more prosperous America. Tonight also happens to be our FEC deadline for February. So before I address the American people tonight, will you make a generous campaign contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $20, $10, or $5 before 8:30 PM EST? I directed my campaign staff to give me a list of the many great citizens who supported me before I speak to the American people at 9 PM EST TONIGHT. CONTRIBUTE $100 CONTRIBUTE $50 CONTRIBUTE $35 CONTRIBUTE $25 CONTRIBUTE OTHER AMOUNT Tonight is our moment to announce our plan for America to the entire world. We will secure our borders and STOP illegal immigration. We will rebuild our depleted military, but follow a new foreign policy that finally puts America FIRST. We will repeal and replace ObamaCare and reform our broken tax system. We will bring our jobs back and make America open for business again. And we WILL MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! Please make a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $20, $10, or $5 toward our end-of-month FEC deadline before 8:30 PM EST TONIGHT to get your name on the my list of contributors. Thank you and God bless you, Donald J. Trump President of the United States

name: Tom Perez
subject: 60,000 donations so we can hit the ground running:
body:
Let's get to work.
---
**Pitch in before midnight tonight to help us reach our 60,000 donation goal:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c5e6/4067b0a/2d4a31ce0/7e7cfe1a/2261762114/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/February-Goal-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170228_tp_nd&source=em_20170228_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170228_tp_nd")****
Friend --

There's no honeymoon period when it comes to getting our party recharged and ready for our fights ahead.

I'm still learning names and figuring out where the best coffee in DNC HQ is, but before I get settled, I need to ask for your help. **[Can you help us hit our goal of 60,000 grassroots donations before our end-of-month deadline tonight so we're ready to hit the ground running?](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c5e6/4067b0a/2d4a31ce0/7e7cfe18/2261762114/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VFgwTlZVMVJQVFY5R1NVVk1SRnR6YkhWblBXdGxlV052WkdWZEpTVT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://my.democrats.org/February-Goal-AB?keycode=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+Can+you+help+us+hit+our+goal+of+60000+gr&utm_campaign=em_20170228_tp_nd&source=em_20170228_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170228_tp_nd")**

I've spent the last few months on a listening tour and at DNC Future Forums all across the country where Democrats have made their voices heard about what we want and need to see from this party moving forward. We only succeed when we have a real presence in every state and territory, and elect people from the school board to the Senate who share our values.

Keith Ellison and I and the rest of the newly elected officers of the Democratic National Committee are fired up and ready to take on Trump and the GOP by communicating our values loud and clear and investing in Democrats from the grassroots on up.

But we need your help. Can you pitch in $3 or whatever you can today to help us start this new chapter off strong?

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

name: Shannon Jackson, Our Revolution
subject: Thank you for protecting health care
body:
Our movement isn't made up of billionaires and special interests - it's made of people like you.
#
_" We had an amazing rally in Huntington, NY. Over 350 people showed up, filling the main hall, a spill over room, and others simply could not get in." \-- Ron Widelec, Our Revolution Member_
Sisters and Brothers,
Across the country this weekend, you showed up and made your voices heard. From one coast to another and everywhere in between, more than 30,000 of us at 152 different rallies stood as one to protect Medicare, Medicaid, Social Security and health care as a right. And our efforts are making a difference.
Support for the Affordable Care Act has reached an all-time high, with a recent Pew Research survey showing that 54% of the American people support the landmark health care law. We cannot afford to go backwards by throwing 30 million Americans off of health insurance.
[**More people are joining this movement every day to fight for a progressive agenda. Make sure you 're at the next rally with your local chapter of Our Revolution.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0a74/43f17890/219e3932/62002c5d/2359918623/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/volunteer-with-our-revolution?source=em170228-full")
We understand that in the wealthiest country in the history of the world, we must cut health care costs for the middle class and working families. **That 's why our demonstrations this weekend were so important.**
The power of public opinion is influencing the debate in Washington and state capitals as we speak. President Trump and his special interest friends, embattled by massive demonstrations like ours this weekend, have not been able to move forward with their plan to give tax breaks to millionaires and billionaires to the detriment of working Americans.
Trump has indicated that he will not cut entitlement programs like Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security in the coming federal budget. But given his history of skirting the truth, we know that we cannot take Mr. Trump at his word -- that's why we have to keep the pressure on.

Dear Friend,

President Trump may have the biggest microphone in Washington, but he doesn't have the only one. Senate Democrats are using every bit of power we have to speak out against his terrible nominees, his very terrible policies, and his very, very terrible attacks on our core values.

**And every contribution we receive from a grassroots supporter like you turns up the volume on our microphone.**

**[Click here to turn up the volume by standing with Democratic Senators today!](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/grassroots-resistance-goal?refcode=DPE-20170228-Franken-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**

No, clicking on that link won't immediately cause Trump's Twitter feed to vanish into the digital abyss. But that doesn't mean it's an insignificant act of resistance.

**You see, when it comes to Trump 's agenda, Senate Democrats are the last, best line of defense. If he wants to trample on President Obama's progressive legacy, he's going to have to go through us first.**

Which is exactly what he and the far right plan to do. Last month, we got outraised by the National Republican Senatorial Committee. Trump and his pals know that if they silence us, there will be no one left to speak up against whatever Trump is planning next.

**But they can 't silence us if supporters like you take action right now.** By contributing a few dollars, you'll help amplify our message of resistance -- and you'll send a loud message of your own to Trump and his right-wing allies: _You 'll have to go through **me** , too._

**[Send a message -- and help amplify ours -- by clicking here and making a contribution right now (your gift will be triple-matched)!](https://secure.dscc. Thanks for reading -- and thanks for clicking.

Al

Try it: Click here and make a contribution to help us fight back against Trump!**](https://secure.dscc. body:
I will be speaking directly to YOU. |

Friend, Tonight at 9 PM EST I’m going to speak before Congress for the first time. But more importantly, I will be speaking to you -- the American people. My speech will be a message of optimism, hope, and love for the greatest country in history. I will lay out our agenda for a stronger, freer, and more prosperous America. Tonight also happens to be our FEC deadline for February. So before I address the American people tonight, will you make a generous campaign contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $20, $10, or $5 before 8:30 PM EST? I directed my campaign staff to give me a list of the many great citizens who supported me before I speak to the American people at 9 PM EST TONIGHT. CONTRIBUTE $100 CONTRIBUTE $50 CONTRIBUTE $35 CONTRIBUTE $25 CONTRIBUTE OTHER AMOUNT Tonight is our moment to announce our plan for America to the entire world. We will secure our borders and STOP illegal immigration. We will rebuild our depleted military, but follow a new foreign policy that finally puts America FIRST. We will repeal and replace ObamaCare and reform our broken tax system. We will bring our jobs back and make America open for business again. And we WILL MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! Please make a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $20, $10, or $5 toward our end-of-month FEC deadline before 8:30 PM EST TONIGHT to get your name on the my list of contributors. name: Keith Ellison
subject: I need your help
body:
Let's get to work.
---
**Pitch in before midnight tonight to help us reach our 60,000 donation goal:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c5e5/4067b0a/2d4619f0f/7e7ceb5d/115300403/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/February-Goal-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170228_ke_nd&source=em_20170228_ke_nd&refcode=em_20170228_ke_nd")****
I've never had any doubt in my mind about what it means to be a Democrat.

We're the party of everyday people in every zip code and from every background. We stand with working people who deserve a living wage. We fight to protect health care as a right that is guaranteed to every American. We demand that corporations and the wealthy pay their fair share.

But although our ideas are right and our values are just, we didn't motivate enough people to get to the polls in 2016. And because of that, our party suffered some heartbreaking defeats.

Today, that all changes. We're on the path back to standing up for every single American.

It's going to take all of us coming together to take back our country -- that's why I was proud to stand with Tom Perez and accept the job of Deputy Chair of the Democratic Party. **[If you're with us, pitch in $3 or more before midnight tonight to show you're ready to rebuild.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c5e5/4067b0a/2d4619f0f/7e7ceb5b/115300403/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VFgwTlZVMVJQVFY5R1NVVk1SRnR6YkhWblBXdGxlV052WkdWZEpTVT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://my.democrats.org/February-Goal-AB?keycode=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+If+youre+with+us+pitch+in+3+or+more+befo&utm_campaign=em_20170228_ke_nd&source=em_20170228_ke_nd&refcode=em_20170228_ke_nd")**

Our pledge to you is that the Democratic Party will leave no stone unturned. We're going to organize in all 50 states, all 3,143 counties. We will compete for every vote and give every Democratic voter a reason to show up at the ballot box.

Together, we're going to win back school boards, state legislatures, and Congress. And we'll beat Trump and take back the White House.

Tom and I know we can do it, because we've both seen what Democrats can accomplish when we work together towards a common goal.

But we don't have a person to spare or a moment to waste, so I need you to have some skin in the game. We're looking for 60,000 grassroots donations before the February deadline at midnight. Chip in $3 or more before then to help Democrats get ready for the fights ahead.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

We're the party of everyday people in every zip code and from every background. We stand with working people who deserve a living wage. We fight to protect health care as a right that is guaranteed to every American. We demand that corporations and the wealthy pay their fair share. But although our ideas are right and our values are just, we didn't motivate enough people to get to the polls in 2016. And because of that, our party suffered some heartbreaking defeats. Today, that all changes. We're on the path back to standing up for every single American. It's going to take all of us coming together to take back our country -- that's why I was proud to stand with Tom Perez and accept the job of Deputy Chair of the Democratic Party. body:
If we have learned anything over the last few weeks, | __ Friend -

If we have learned anything over the last few weeks, it's the importance of grassroots organizing. Every time the Trump administration has acted, we--the American people--have been there to stand up for our values.

And these efforts are having impact. Over the February recess Republicans went home to face tough questions and angry constituents at town hall meetings across the country. And when they returned to Washington yesterday they found their plan to dismantle the Affordable Care Act starting to unravel.

But this is only the beginning. We can't back down now. [**That 's why I am turning to you to help us get ready for the big fights ahead. Support from grassroots activists like you helps us keep the temperature up and expand our reach to more and more people who want to be part of the resistance to Trump's extremist agenda. **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b07/70cdc2ba/474e05da/397e7e2/1954824460/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh_feb2018?refcode=em022817&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Thatrsquos+why+I+am+turning+to+you+to+he&utm_campaign=em022817&source=em022817")

Resistance isn't easy -- it requires commitment, patience and numbers. We won't be able to create a world without Trump's twitter feed overnight. But each day Donald Trump is moving closer to his own political abyss with the American people and we can't let the Koch Brothers and others drown out the real news about what his reckless agenda means for our country.

Tonight, Trump will present a plan that cuts key investments in our future, guts several federal agencies, and calls for repeal of the Affordable Care Act and defunding of Planned Parenthood.

Our message of resistance needs to be as strong as it can be. [**Send a message -- and help amplify ours -- by clicking here and making a contribution right now!**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b07/70cdc2ba/474e05da/397e7fa/1954824460/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh_feb2018?refcode=em022817&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=9+-+Send+a+message++and+help+amplify+ours++b&utm_campaign=em022817&source=em022817")

We won't win every fight, but we can and will push back hard as Donald Trump attacks the values that make our country great.

Thanks for your activism -- and for helping us fight back,

Chris

"" If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: "" "" "" "" "" "" "" Resistance isn't easy -- it requires commitment, patience and numbers. We won't be able to create a world without Trump's twitter feed overnight. But each day Donald Trump is moving closer to his own political abyss with the American people and we can't let the Koch Brothers and others drown out the real news about what his reckless agenda means for our country. Tonight, Trump will present a plan that cuts key investments in our future, guts several federal agencies, and calls for repeal of the Affordable Care Act and defunding of Planned Parenthood. Our message of resistance needs to be as strong as it can be. "" We won't win every fight, but we can and will push back hard as Donald Trump attacks the values that make our country great. Thanks for your activism -- and for helping us fight back, Chris Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate "" We believe that emails are a vital way to stay in direct contact with supporters. You're receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com. "" if you'd like to unsubscribe from these messages. name: Steve Beshear
subject: I know the Affordable Care Act works
body:
Donald Trump and Republicans are wrong about health care **Pitch in whatever you can to show the GOP we're ready to fight back.
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c5f6/4067b0a/2d3ca0473/7e7cc558/3729496885/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170228_sb_nd&source=em_20170228_sb_nd&refcode=em_20170228_sb_nd")****
Friend --

Real leaders don't spread derision and division -- they build partnerships and offer solutions instead of ideology and blame.

And they make improving people's lives their highest priority.

That's the message I delivered in response to Donald Trump's address to Congress tonight.

We heard throughout his campaign as he promised that making life better for the working men and women of this country would be his priority. The only problem is the GOP's agenda is fundamentally at odds with the best interests of those Americans.

I'm proud to be a Democrat because I know that when our party is firing on all cylinders, we have what it takes to help people in big, meaningful ways. Our policies and our priorities are the ones that make a difference in the lives of the greatest number of people in this country -- not just the 1% who stand to benefit from Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and Paul Ryan's plans.

As Democrats, our job is to make that case. [**Can I count on you to help us do it?**](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c5f6/4067b0a/2d3ca0473/7e7cc559/3729496885/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+CUSTOMDATASETslugwhitelistcommaletterkey&utm_campaign=em_20170228_sb_nd&source=em_20170228_sb_nd&refcode=em_20170228_sb_nd")

When I was governor of the red state of Kentucky, I saw firsthand the disconnect between what people think Democrats stand for in Washington and the good work we were doing on the ground. My state had some of the most unhealthy people in this country for as long as they have been keeping rankings. We needed a big solution. Lo and behold, the Affordable Care Act came along, and I saw it as our one chance to make a big difference in people's futures.

Kentucky is not a rich state, and I was worried if we could afford to do it. I found out that we couldn't afford _not_ to do it. Independent, third-party projections showed that over the next eight years, it would infuse $15 billion into our state's economy, create 17,000 new jobs, and have a positive impact on our budget.

I would tell many skeptical Kentuckians, "Look, you don't have to like the president, and you don't have to like me -- because this is not about him or me. It's about you. It's about your family. It's about your kids." We showed how it would help us economically. How if we had a healthy workforce, we could have a productive workforce.

In the first 18 months, we had almost half a million people sign up for coverage -- many of whom were getting health care for the first time in their lives. We saw one of the largest drops in the uninsured rate in the country, from 20 to 7.5 percent. And a study of the first year of expanded Medicaid showed a positive economic impact on Kentucky's economy and state budget that was even higher than anticipated.

My successor in office was a Republican who ran and won on the promise of ripping up Obamacare on day one. But we saw in Kentucky that when repeal rhetoric meets repeal reality -- reality wins. Rather than killing a policy that was working for so many, Governor Bevin instead made alterations with some conservative window dressing while leaving core elements and benefits in place. Just like a governor named Mike Pence did in Indiana. But the ACA is still under attack, and families are still at risk.

American families desperately need our president to put his full attention on creating opportunity and good-paying jobs and preserving their right to affordable health care and a quality education. It's up to Democrats to keep making our case and keep that pressure on.

Pitch in $3 or more tonight to help our party take this fight to all 50 states -- and win it.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

clean_body: Donald Trump and Republicans are wrong about health care Pitch in whatever you can to show the GOP we're ready to fight back. ""** Friend -- Real leaders don't spread derision and division -- they build partnerships and offer solutions instead of ideology and blame. And they make improving people's lives their highest priority. That's the message I delivered in response to Donald Trump's address to Congress tonight. We heard throughout his campaign as he promised that making life better for the working men and women of this country would be his priority. The only problem is the GOP's agenda is fundamentally at odds with the best interests of those Americans. I'm proud to be a Democrat because I know that when our party is firing on all cylinders, we have what it takes to help people in big, meaningful ways. Our policies and our priorities are the ones that make a difference in the lives of the greatest number of people in this country -- not just the 1% who stand to benefit from Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and Paul Ryan's plans. As Democrats, our job is to make that case. "" When I was governor of the red state of Kentucky, I saw firsthand the disconnect between what people think Democrats stand for in Washington and the good work we were doing on the ground. My state had some of the most unhealthy people in this country for as long as they have been keeping rankings. We needed a big solution. Lo and behold, the Affordable Care Act came along, and I saw it as our one chance to make a big difference in people's futures. Kentucky is not a rich state, and I was worried if we could afford to do it. I found out that we couldn't afford not to do it. Independent, third-party projections showed that over the next eight years, it would infuse $15 billion into our state's economy, create 17,000 new jobs, and have a positive impact on our budget. I would tell many skeptical Kentuckians, "Look, you don't have to like the president, and you don't have to like me -- because this is not about him or me. It's about you. It's about your family. It's about your kids." We showed how it would help us economically. How if we had a healthy workforce, we could have a productive workforce. In the first 18 months, we had almost half a million people sign up for coverage -- many of whom were getting health care for the first time in their lives. We saw one of the largest drops in the uninsured rate in the country, from 20 to 7.5 percent. And a study of the first year of expanded Medicaid showed a positive economic impact on Kentucky's economy and state budget that was even higher than anticipated. My successor in office was a Republican who ran and won on the promise of ripping up Obamacare on day one. But we saw in Kentucky that when repeal rhetoric meets repeal reality -- reality wins. Rather than killing a policy that was working for so many, Governor Bevin instead made alterations with some conservative window dressing while leaving core elements and benefits in place. Just like a governor named Mike Pence did in Indiana. But the ACA is still under attack, and families are still at risk. American families desperately need our president to put his full attention on creating opportunity and good-paying jobs and preserving their right to affordable health care and a quality education. It's up to Democrats to keep making our case and keep that pressure on. Pitch in $3 or more tonight to help our party take this fight to all 50 states -- and win it. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.
